answers: Kanban = visual workflow management. Tasks move through columns: To Do → In Progress → Done. Simple and effective.
Kanban principles: visualize work, limit WIP (work in progress), manage flow, make policies explicit, improve continuously.
Kanban vs Scrum: Kanban has no sprints (continuous flow), no fixed roles. Scrum has time-boxed sprints and defined roles.
Start Kanban: create a board (physical or Trello/Jira), define columns for your workflow, and limit WIP to 2-3 items per person.

feelings.question: kanban board ki?
feelings.answer: Yes, Kanban boards are visual workflow management tools that help teams visualize their work in progress (WIP) and manage it effectively. They have no sprints or fixed roles, making them flexible and effective for continuous improvement.
